title: Mollugo glinus
taxonomy: Mollugo glinus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Molluginaceae, commonly known as carpetweed or common carpetweed. It belongs to the genus Mollugo, which consists of several species of small, annual or perennial plants. The family Molluginaceae is a small family of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Mollugo glinus is a cosmopolitan species, found in many parts of the world, including North America, Europe, Asia, and Africa. Its classification is further supported by its morphological characteristics and genetic studies.

intro: Mollugo glinus, also known as carpetweed or Mollugo, is a perennial weed plant native to Asia and Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Mollugo glinus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Mollugo glinus is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make teas, capsules, and tinctures for the treatment of various health conditions.
